metadata.description: Collection of AI agent skills for AWS CDK development, AWS documentation search, AWS Athena SQL querying, CloudWatch Logs Insights querying, Anki flashcard management, Obsidian vault management, audio conversion, subtitle/caption conversion, podcast generation, safe terminal command registration, image generation, ML system design interviews, and markdown note search

plugins.name: aws-documentation
plugins.description: This plugin contains skills for AWS CDK infrastructure development and searching official AWS documentation. Use for CDK guidance, architecture patterns, GenAI constructs, Bedrock agent schemas, CDK Nag rules, documentation search, and AWS architecture recommendations.

plugins.name: cron-restoration-guard
plugins.description: This plugin contains a SessionStart hook that fires on session resume events. It instructs the agent to verify that cron jobs registered in the previous session are still active and to re-register any that are missing.

plugins.name: convert-audio
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for converting audio files between formats using ffmpeg (MP3, WAV, AAC, FLAC, Opus, OGG), adjusting bitrate/quality, changing sample rate/channels, modifying playback speed, or adding/editing audio metadata (ID3 tags).

plugins.name: local-skill-issues-tracker
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for a local JSON-based issue tracker where disconnected agents report skill bugs and feature requests. Includes a filesystem watcher with cmux integration for live notifications on new issues, status changes, and comments.

plugins.name: anki
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for working with Anki flashcard software through the AnkiConnect API. Use for creating flashcards, managing decks, searching notes/cards, syncing collections, controlling review sessions, or any Anki automation tasks.

plugins.name: obsidian
plugins.description: This plugin contains four skills for Obsidian: CLI operations (use-obsidian-cli) for creating, reading, searching, and managing notes via the Obsidian CLI; Obsidian Flavored Markdown syntax (use-obsidian-markdown) covering wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, math, Mermaid, and more; personal knowledge management methodology (manage-personal-knowledge-in-obsidian) with atomic notes philosophy, linking strategies, tagging conventions, and structured workflows; and vault enrichment (enrich-obsidian-notes-with-best-practices) for adding Best Practices and Anti-Patterns sections to knowledge notes using parallel sub-agents and internet research.

plugins.name: aws-athena
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for executing AWS Athena SQL queries, downloading results from S3, running queries in parallel, and optimizing query performance with Common Table Expressions (CTEs).

plugins.name: aws-dynamodb
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for managing the full DynamoDB export lifecycle: exporting tables to S3 via ExportTableToPointInTime, converting DynamoDB JSON to Parquet with AWS Glue or PySpark, filtering exported data with Athena or Glue predicates, and sharing datasets cross-account with scoped S3 bucket policies.

plugins.name: aws-glue
plugins.description: This plugin contains skills for AWS Glue ETL jobs and workflows: writing, configuring, debugging, and monitoring Glue jobs with update-job gotchas, worker type sizing, CloudWatch and Observability metrics, S3 shuffle, per-worker progress reporting, API call tracking via ApiCallTracker, live job monitoring with long-poll-with-exit, cmux-keystrokes, and tmux-keystrokes modes, resilient credential handling, VPC endpoint validation, Flex job cost savings, Spark UI setup, small files (groupFiles/coalesce), timeout anti-patterns, MaxConcurrentRuns gotchas, a troubleshooting quick-reference, and live workflow monitoring with per-node job/crawler state tracking.

plugins.name: aws-cloudwatch
plugins.description: This plugin contains skills for Amazon CloudWatch: querying CloudWatch Logs with Log Insights (real-time progress tracking, flexible time ranges, multiple output formats, multi-log-group support); and publishing, querying, and designing monitoring with CloudWatch custom metrics (PutMetricData, EMF, GetMetricData, alarms, composite alarms, metric math, dimension cardinality, cost optimization, high-resolution metrics, metric streams, Contributor Insights).

plugins.name: aws-support
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for monitoring AWS Support cases for status changes, severity changes, and new communications with long-poll, cmux, and tmux delivery modes. Requires Business or Enterprise AWS support plan.

plugins.name: block-main-repo-writes
plugins.description: This plugin contains a PreToolUse hook that blocks Write/Edit/NotebookEdit on files inside the main git checkout when the CC_HOOK_BLOCK_WRITING_TO_MAIN_REPO environment variable is set, forcing all edits to happen inside git worktrees. Optimized to cost nothing when the env var is unset.

plugins.name: aws-quota-service
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for monitoring AWS Service Quotas increase requests for status changes, with approval/denial notifications across regions.

plugins.name: protect-file-system-access
plugins.description: This plugin contains a hook that blocks direct editing of protected system files including AWS credentials, SSH keys, shell profiles, and package lockfiles. Use to prevent accidental corruption of critical configuration files.

plugins.name: pyspark
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for writing, debugging, and reviewing PySpark scripts and Spark DataFrame transformations. Covers anti-patterns (JSON inference OOM, data skew, shuffle spill, Python UDFs), coding style (import aliases, type hints, method chains, join hygiene, null handling), and Spark tuning (AQE, broadcast joins, shuffle partitions).

plugins.name: qmd
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for searching markdown notes on-device using the QMD CLI. QMD combines BM25 full-text search, vector semantic search, and LLM re-ranking for hybrid retrieval across indexed markdown collections. Use for finding information in local knowledge bases, retrieving documents by path or docid, running structured queries with lex:/vec:/hyde: typed lines, and managing indexed collections.

plugins.name: claude-code-status-line
plugins.description: This plugin configures Claude Code to use a rich statusline script that displays context usage, token counts, cost, session duration, model ID, effort level, git branch, and working directory. A SessionStart hook checks if the statusline is already configured and updates user-level settings if not.

plugins.name: register-safe-terminal-commands
plugins.description: This plugin auto-syncs safe terminal commands to Claude Code settings on session start, and contains a skill for manual sync with dry-run and verbose options. Use when configuring which bash commands Claude Code can execute without requiring approval.

plugins.name: podcast-generation
plugins.description: This plugin contains skills for AI-powered podcast production: generating podcast scripts with natural conversation flow and converting scripts into high-quality audio using text-to-speech synthesis on AWS. Use when creating podcast scripts, multi-speaker dialogue, or generating podcast audio from scripts.

plugins.name: communication
plugins.description: This plugin contains skills for AI agents communicating and writing emails. Covers async channel etiquette (message value test, signing conventions, message style, frequency, epistemic responsibility, anti-patterns, channel-type guidelines) and professional email writing (subject lines, tone calibration, difficult scenarios, follow-up strategy, AI prompting for emails).

plugins.name: cli-design
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for designing and reviewing command-line interfaces (CLIs). Covers language-agnostic best practices, POSIX/GNU conventions, anti-patterns, output formatting, error messages, exit codes, configuration, testing, versioning, performance, distribution, and AI-agent-friendly CLI design.

plugins.name: cmux-terminal
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for orchestrating terminal sessions, browser automation, and progress reporting inside the cmux terminal multiplexer. Also includes a SessionStart hook that detects whether the session is running in cmux and prints workspace/surface context and LLM behavioural instructions.

plugins.name: file-system-tools
plugins.description: This plugin contains skills for freeing disk space on developer machines, scanning for bloat directories, and safely analyzing large files without exceeding the context window. Cleans package manager caches, IDE build artefacts, and Docker resources. Scans for node_modules, virtual environments, and build caches. Covers size-checking strategies, targeted extraction with Bash/Python/Node.js one-liners, structured data analysis (JSON, CSV, XML, logs), and token estimation. Use when disk is full, cleaning caches, reclaiming storage, finding bloat, or working with large files.

plugins.name: generate-image
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for generating images using Amazon Nova Canvas on AWS Bedrock. Use when creating images, illustrations, AI art, text-to-image generation, or visual content. Not for editing existing images.

plugins.name: writing
plugins.description: Writing quality and technical design document skills. write-well covers clarity, structure, conciseness, persuasion, AI writing hygiene, and editing checklists for design docs, RFCs, emails, postmortems, and status updates. write-technical-design guides HLD/LLD drafting section-by-section with templates, architecture diagram standards, assembly checklists, and red-flag detection.

plugins.name: ml-system-design-interviewer
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for conducting, evaluating, or preparing for ML System Design interviews for Principal/Staff-level engineering candidates. Use when running mock interviews, preparing questions, or assessing candidates with structured rubrics.

plugins.name: skill-management
plugins.description: This plugin contains skills for skill development and quality assurance: portability best practices, file naming conventions, the ${SKILL_DIR} placeholder, context management, self-containment rules (skill-creator-extra-tips); customer-perspective skill evaluation using synthetic tasks to find bugs by executing skills as a real user would (evaluate-skills-with-synthetic-tasks); single-skill and bulk plugin quality reviews with parallel sub-agents and auto-filed issues (review-skill); and enriching existing skills with new knowledge from external sources such as official docs, post-mortems, and code examples (enrich-skill-via-research).

plugins.name: subtitle-tools
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for converting WebVTT caption/subtitle files (.vtt) to deduplicated plaintext. Strips inline tags, drops cue headers, and collapses the overlapping-cue repetition common in auto-generated captions. Supports optional [HH:MM:SS] timestamp prefixes.

plugins.name: toon
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for converting between JSON and TOON (Token-Oriented Object Notation) formats using the toon CLI. TOON is a compact, human-readable format that encodes the JSON data model with minimal tokens, designed as a translation layer for LLM input. Use for encoding JSON to TOON, decoding TOON to JSON, measuring token savings with --stats, or reducing token usage when feeding data to language models.

plugins.name: update-versioned-permissions
plugins.description: This plugin contains a SessionStart hook that auto-clones stale versioned plugin path entries in Claude Code permissions when plugins are upgraded. For each permission entry referencing an old plugin version, it adds a matching entry for the currently installed version. Additive-only — never removes existing entries. Also updates statusLine.command to the latest installed version if stale.

plugins.name: claude-code-session-history
plugins.description: This plugin contains a skill for analyzing Claude Code session JSONL transcripts to identify recurring patterns, skill gaps, agent failure modes, and improvement opportunities. Covers transcript storage layout, JSONL entry types, user message extraction recipe, sub-agent dispatch for large volumes, and output format for pattern reporting.
